refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple requirements or specify implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety compatibility criteria, such as p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency
Use a dedicated communication channel with latency monitoring and adaptive control
Incorporate predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules could increase coupling
Shared resources causing contention may introduce latency variability
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a modular design with clear separation between communication handling and control logic supports independence and easier latency management.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Latency measurement and monitoring are accurate and timely
Predictive control algorithms may be needed if latency approaches upper limits
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms in the DP to handle variable network conditions and ensure consistent teleoperation safety.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control system including prioritized data handling to minimize latency

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional telemetry data exchange for control and feedback

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Incoming task commands for teleoperation control

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication link for data transmission with latency considerations

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but with high diffusion and low cohesion due to encompassing many unrelated functions.
Vehicle module focuses on physical vehicle systems; teleoperation control logic would diffuse heavily outside this boundary.
Highly cohesive containment of teleoperation control system and prioritized data handling; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Safety services are related but distinct; teleoperation control logic would not be well contained here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary that fully contains the DP of designing a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency, minimizing diffusion and coupling outside this module.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Telemetry interfaces may require careful design to avoid latency overhead.
Coordination with Safety Services for safety alerts may cause some cross-module dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency, which aligns with the requirement for end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation. The acceptance criterion of p95 latency ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions is supported by the DP's focus on latency minimization.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Telemetry interfaces may require careful design to avoid latency overhead.
Coordination with Safety Services for safety alerts may cause some cross-module dependencies.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module has high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion of the DP within this IM boundary. Other modules show higher diffusion and lower containment, confirming that Teleoperation is the best architectural packaging for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring the end-to-end latency of the Teleoperation module under the specified network conditions and ensuring it meets the p95 ≤ 150 ms criterion.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The DP is clearly allocated to the Teleoperation module, which owns the prioritized data handling and teleoperation control logic, providing clear ownership and responsibility for meeting the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface design strategies to minimize latency overhead at Network Link and Telemetry interfaces to mitigate diffusion risks.
Define clear coordination protocols with Safety Services to manage cross-module dependencies without impacting latency.
Consider adding monitoring or diagnostic capabilities within the Teleoperation module to verify latency performance against acceptance criteria during operation.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed indicating that the current DP and IM adequately address the FR with feasible verification and clear allocation, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks at interfaces and cross-module dependencies necessitate explicit documentation and design refinement to mitigate latency overhead and ensure robust coordination. This revision will strengthen the design before proceeding to further decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface design strategies minimizing latency overhead, define clear coordination protocols with Safety Services, and consider adding monitoring capabilities within the Teleoperation module to verify latency performance, thereby addressing diffusion risks and enhancing safety assurance.

assumptions: The current DP and IM sufficiently meet the FR requirements under agreed network conditions.
Latency measurement and verification methods are available and feasible within the Teleoperation module.
open_risks: Potential latency overhead due to interface complexity with Network Link and Telemetry interfaces.
Cross-module dependencies with Safety Services may impact latency and safety performance.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface design documentation demonstrating latency minimization strategies.
Defined coordination protocols with Safety Services.
Monitoring or diagnostic capability specifications and validation plans within the Teleoperation module.
